mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate held near the zero lower bound compresses the equity discount rate and supports valuation multiples, producing persistent positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon as long as accommodative policy is maintained.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and 60-day forward SPX return falls below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window, or SPX underperforms cash returns despite near-zero rates persisting for 6+ months.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate of more than 0.3 percentage points over a trailing 3-month window signals accelerating labor market recovery, which raises expected corporate earnings and drives S&P 500 forward returns higher over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 6.2% Feb 2021, down from pandemic peak
failure_condition: In more than 50% of historical instances where UNRATE fell > 0.3pp over 3 months, 20-day forward SPX returns are flat or negative, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates broad-based consumer demand strength financed by stimulus and low rates, which jointly signal an above-trend growth impulse that equity markets price in over the following month; neither series alone is sufficient because RSAFS can spike from transfers alone while HOUST confirms durable investment demand.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS m/m growth > 1% AND HOUST m/m growth > 3% simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 568215M and HOUST at 1580K as of early 2021, both elevated post-stimulus
failure_condition: Joint-trigger episodes produce 20-day forward SPX returns not significantly different from unconditional baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or interaction term adds no incremental Rank IC versus either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in Industrial Production (INDPRO) reflect improving capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, which forward-predicts short-horizon equity gains as investors extrapolate recovering output into near-term earnings guidance over the next trading week.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 107.18 as of Jan 2021, recovering from 2020 trough
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns show no statistically significant correlation with monthly INDPRO changes (p > 0.10) across the full sample, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any rolling 1-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains pinned near zero, markets price in future policy tightening risk and rising real yields, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month; this mechanism is specific to a reflation regime where inflation expectations are re-anchoring upward from a low base.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 263.161 Feb 2021 with FEDFUNDS at 0.08%
regime_hint: post-COVID reflation regime with zero interest rate policy
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the inflation-acceleration signal, or Rank IC flips sign across two consecutive rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) and decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) jointly confirm a genuine, non-noisy labor market recovery that supports consumption and corporate revenue outlooks, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon; either series alone is subject to revision noise or composition effects that this interaction filters out.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS m/m increase > 200k AND UNRATE decline > 0.1pp in same month
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 143,048k and UNRATE at 6.2% Feb 2021
failure_condition: Joint-trigger months fail to produce 60-day forward SPX returns above unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC contribution from the interaction term is not significant relative to single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rebound in real GDP (GDPC1) quarter-over-quarter annualized growth above 5% signals that aggregate demand has crossed a threshold consistent with full-cycle recovery, prompting equity markets to reprice growth expectations upward and delivering positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following month.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 18,783.9B as of Q4 2020, rebounding from 2020 contraction
failure_condition: Quarters with GDPC1 growth > 5% show subsequent 20-day forward SPX returns not significantly different from baseline, or the effect reverses in more than 40% of historical high-growth quarters due to prior market anticipation.

mechanism_narrative: In an early-cycle recovery regime, when Industrial Production (INDPRO) growth outpaces nonfarm payroll (PAYEMS) growth by a wide margin, it indicates output gains driven by productivity or automation rather than labor rehiring, signaling supply-chain and capacity strain that raises input cost pressure and compresses margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO m/m growth exceeds PAYEMS m/m growth by more than 2 standard deviations
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO recovering to 107.18 while PAYEMS still well below pre-pandemic levels as of early 2021
regime_hint: early-cycle recovery with labor market lag
failure_condition: Divergence episodes between INDPRO and PAYEMS growth do not produce negative 20-day forward SPX returns in at least 55% of historical instances, or Rank IC is statistically indistinguishable from zero over any 3-year rolling window.
